pr-evidence/current-benchmark-status/pr-.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 review found no blocking issues. The reviewer verified the snapshot matches tracked benchmarks/databricks artifacts, the g6/L4 strict target versus g5/A10G compatibility distinction is clear, the benchmark/pr-evidence/databricks-runs artifact boundary is preserved, release evidence claims remain scoped to tracked benchmark/storage/native probe artifacts, and no Databricks runs were launched.
verification: poetry run pytest -q tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_pr_evidence.py -> 71 passed
poetry check && poetry check --lock -> All set
GPT-5.5 reviewer approved PR #436 with no blocking issues; GitHub rejected recording formal approval as self-review
what_changed: Added benchmarks/databricks/CURRENT.md as the human-readable entry point for the current Cachet Databricks benchmark snapshot.
Linked the current snapshot from the root README and benchmark README files.
Extended governance coverage so the standalone benchmark area keeps g6/L4 target, g5/A10G compatibility, baseline/cache arms, and benchmark/pr-evidence/databricks-runs boundaries explicit.
pr-evidence/current-benchmark-status/pr-.why: The current benchmark JSON and per-run README files were tracked, but readers still needed to know which folder represented the current release picture. This PR makes benchmark status discoverable without asking humans to inspect pr-evidence or local scratch bundles.
